seo.title: Mastra Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Mastra agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using Mastra for AI-driven hiring workflows face heightened compliance risks due to strict regulations like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act, which classify hiring tools as high-risk AI systems. Without proper guardrails, unvalidated workflow outputs and stale memory retrieval can lead to discriminatory decisions based on protected characteristics. Vex provides essential middleware to validate agent outputs, ensuring fairness, transparency, and auditability in every hiring decision.

complianceRequirements.description: AI screening tools must not discriminate based on race, gender, age, or other protected characteristics and require annual bias audits.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates resume screening outputs against bias patterns and generates audit logs for EEOC compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Employers must notify candidates when AI is used in hiring decisions and conduct annual bias audits.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex automatically inserts AI disclosure notices and tracks audit trails for every candidate interaction.

complianceRequirements.description: Hiring AI systems require risk assessments, transparency, and human oversight for decision-making.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces human-in-the-loop validation and provides risk scoring for all agent decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Resume screening must accommodate disabilities and avoid disparate impact on protected groups.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags ableist language patterns and ensures scoring algorithms don't penalize protected characteristics.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Candidate Scoring
industryRisks.description: Workflow transitions pass raw, unfiltered scores that embed proxy discrimination (zip code → race correlations).
industryRisks.example: Agent scores Bay Area candidates 20% higher due to zip code patterns correlating with ethnicity.

industryRisks.name: Stale Memory Injection
industryRisks.description: Old training data injects outdated salary expectations by gender into current evaluations.
industryRisks.example: Memory retrieval surfaces 2018 data showing women negotiate 15% less, biasing offer recommendations.

industryRisks.name: Malformed Tool Outputs
industryRisks.description: Third-party ATS integrations return unstructured data that confuses scoring logic.
industryRisks.example: LinkedIn API returns 'Senior Engineer (NYC)' parsed as location bias against non-NYC candidates.

industryRisks.name: Sync Data Conflicts
industryRisks.description: Real-time applicant tracking conflicts with agent memory, creating inconsistent evaluations.
industryRisks.example: ATS marks candidate as 'withdrawn' but agent memory shows 'top prospect,' triggering dual offers.

implementation.title: Validate Resume Screening Outputs
implementation.description: Add Vex middleware to catch bias in candidate scoring between workflow steps.

implementation.title: AI Disclosure & Audit Logging
implementation.description: Automatically notify candidates and log decisions for NYC Local Law 144 compliance.

implementation.title: Prevent Stale Memory Bias
implementation.description: Validate memory retrieval against current compliance rules before agent reasoning.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Mastra in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
TechCo deployed Mastra to streamline hiring for 500 engineering roles quarterly. The workflow ingested resumes via Lever ATS, scored candidates using a fine-tuned Llama model, and ranked them for recruiter review. Memory stored past hiring patterns to 'learn' ideal profiles, while third-party tools pulled LinkedIn data and salary benchmarks.

Three months in, the EEOC flagged disparate impact: female candidates scored 18% lower despite equal qualifications. Investigation revealed zip code proxies for race in memory retrieval, unvalidated salary data embedding gender gaps, and workflow transitions passing raw scores without bias checks. NYC Local Law 144 violations surfaced when candidates sued over undisclosed AI screening.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex middleware integration took 2 days. Resume scoring validation blocked 97% of biased outputs, memory sanitization removed 23% of discriminatory patterns, and automatic disclosures satisfied regulators. Annual bias audits dropped from 40 hours to 4 hours via Vex dashboards. TechCo avoided $2.7M in penalties, diversity hiring improved 32%, and recruiter trust in AI increased from 41% to 89%.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Mastra workflows today and eliminate hiring compliance risks.
